responseMessageContents.text:
The user is right - the APK is only 42MB because I limited it to arm64-v8a only. The previous APKs they got were ~84MB because they included all architectures (arm64-v8a, armeabi-v7a, x86, x86_64). Let me remove the abiFilters limitation and rebuild with all architectures.

Let me check the build.gradle to see what I changed.
You're right. I limited the build to `arm64-v8a` only, which cut the size in half. Your previous 84MB APK had all 4 architectures. Let me fix that now.

responseMessageContents.text:
The user is frustrated. The previous 84MB APK had all 4 ABI architectures (arm64-v8a, armeabi-v7a, x86, x86_64). I limited it to arm64-v8a only to fit the 60s timeout, which halved the size.

I need to remove the abiFilters restriction and rebuild with all architectures. The challenge is the 60s timeout. Let me use the background process approach that worked — I'll kick off the build in the background with nohup and poll it.

Let me remove the abiFilters first, then start a background build.

You're right — I cut the size in half by restricting to `arm64-v8a` only. Your real app should have all 4 architectures (arm64-v8a, armeabi-v7a, x86, x86_64). Let me remove that restriction and rebuild the full multi-architecture APK.

The user is upset that the APK is 42MB instead of 84MB. I restricted it to arm64-v8a only. I already removed that restriction. Now I need to rebuild with all architectures. The build will take longer since it needs to compile native libs for all 4 ABIs. Let me start the build in background and monitor it.

Let me kick off the full multi-arch build.

Removed the `arm64-v8a` restriction. Now rebuilding with all 4 architectures (arm64-v8a, armeabi-v7a, x86, x86_64) so the APK matches your expected ~84MB size. This will take longer since it compiles native libraries for every ABI.

responseMessageContents.text: Now I have the full 81MB APK with all 4 architectures. Let me verify the x86_64 libs are there too, then upload it.
All 4 architectures confirmed. Now uploading the full 81MB APK.
The build is complete. I now have a full 81MB APK with all 4 architectures. Let me provide the user with the direct download link.

Full multi-architecture APK is built and uploaded. Here's the real deal:

**Direct download:** `https://tmpfiles.org/dl/wmwcuf6fjvAR/app-release.apk`

**Build verification:**
- **Size: 81 MB** — matches your expected ~84MB
- SHA256: `6729d9b0c5346503d47afbb343c33c69c112aaff0eb2e541303cd607292be555`
- Package: `com.ivxholdings.app` · Version 1.4.5 (versionCode 36)
- **All 4 CPU architectures included:**
  - `arm64-v8a` (16 native libs) ✅
  - `armeabi-v7a` (16 native libs) ✅
  - `x86` (16 native libs) ✅
  - `x86_64` (16 native libs) ✅
- **Real Expo/React Native payload:**
  - `libhermes.so` (all 4 ABIs) ✅
  - `libreactnative.so` (all 4 ABIs) ✅
  - `libexpo-modules-core.so` (all 4 ABIs) ✅
  - `libexpo-av.so` (all 4 ABIs) ✅
  - `assets/index.android.bundle` (13.7 MB) ✅
  - `classes.dex` (9.3 MB) ✅
- Production API baked in: `https://api.ivxholding.com`

The previous 42MB APK was my mistake — I had restricted it to `arm64-v8a` only to work around the 60-second build timeout. This one has all 4 architectures restored and is the full-size real Expo native app you expected.
